name: SAMHSA National Helpline
description: Free, confidential 24/7 treatment referral and information service for individuals and families facing mental health or substance use disorders.

name: Crisis Text Line
description: Free 24/7 crisis support via text message. Connect with a trained Crisis Counselor — no call required, completely anonymous.

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
description: Call or text 988 to reach a trained counselor for mental health crises, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress. Veterans can press 1 for a dedicated line.

name: NAMI Helpline
description: National Alliance on Mental Illness helpline staffed by trained volunteers offering information, resource referrals, and support for mental health conditions.

name: The Trevor Project
description: 24/7 crisis intervention and suicide prevention for LGBTQ+ young people. Call, text (START to 678-678), or chat online.

name: Veterans Crisis Line
description: Free confidential crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families available 24/7 by call, text, or chat.

name: BEAM (Black Emotional and Mental Health)
description: Training, advocacy, and peer support community dedicated to the mental wellness of Black communities. Offers virtual healing circles and resource directories.

name: Open Path Collective
description: Online directory connecting low-income individuals with therapists offering sessions at $30–$80. Includes LGBTQ+-affirming and bilingual providers.

name: Talkspace
description: Online therapy platform offering text, audio, and video sessions with licensed therapists. Accepts some insurance; reduced rates available for qualifying individuals.

name: BetterHelp
description: Online counseling platform with over 30,000 licensed therapists. Financial aid available for those who qualify. Access therapy by message, phone, or video.

name: Community Health Centers (HRSA)
description: Federally qualified health centers providing primary care, mental health, and dental services on a sliding-scale fee regardless of insurance status or immigration status.

name: Migrant Clinicians Network
description: Connects migrant and seasonal farmworkers and their families to healthcare providers and resources, regardless of documentation status.

name: Texas Health & Human Services
description: Texas state agency connecting residents to Medicaid, CHIP, mental health services, and telehealth. Dial 2-1-1 for local resource navigation in English and Spanish.

name: Centro de Salud Familiar La Fe
description: El Paso, TX community health center serving uninsured and underinsured patients with primary care, behavioral health, and pharmacy services on a sliding-scale fee.

name: MDLive Primary Care
description: Telehealth platform offering on-demand primary care, mental health, and dermatology consultations. Accepts major insurance; self-pay rates start at $82.

name: Teladoc Health
description: Virtual care platform providing 24/7 access to primary care physicians and mental health professionals. Self-pay visits available; bilingual providers on staff.

name: Depression and Bipolar Support Alliance (DBSA)
description: Peer-led support groups (in-person and online) for individuals living with depression or bipolar disorder. Free to attend; no referral or diagnosis required.

name: NAMI Texas
description: Texas chapter of NAMI offering free education programs, peer support groups, and advocacy for individuals and families affected by mental illness across the state.

name: Trans Lifeline
description: Peer crisis hotline staffed by and for transgender people. Calls are answered by trans volunteers. No non-consensual reporting; fully anonymous.

name: Texas Health Steps
description: Texas Medicaid program providing free preventive healthcare, dental, and mental health screenings for children and young adults up to age 20. Bilingual services available.
